definitions.CT1: every claim's evidence resolves in-repo or is marked external with why
definitions.CT10: audit outputs are byte-identical across runs at the same commit (enforced by tests/test_meta_manifest.py and tests/test_meta_assurance.py)
definitions.CT2: every non-retracted claim declares falsification and scope
definitions.CT3: classification in vocabulary; facts have evidence + working reproduce
definitions.CT4: claim<->threat references are bidirectional and resolve
definitions.CT5: every quoted metric matches its source artifact
definitions.CT6: harness commit exists; pyproject/README/manifest versions agree
definitions.CT7: threats carry mitigation + future experiment; resolved needs evidence
definitions.CT8: prose and register threat ID sets are identical
definitions.CT9: claims carry confidence; non-facts name their remaining confound

D01_determinism.note: no register entry tags this dimension

D02_reproducibility.note: no register entry tags this dimension

D05_dataset_discrimination.note: no register entry tags this dimension

no_composite_score: deliberate — a scalar would exceed the evidence (see docs/EVALUATION_QUALITY.md)
